chores.title: Edit + delete "I owe {partner}" debts on the settlement page
chores.description: Partner-debt entries (Expense{paidBy:'gf'}) can be created from the settlement page but never edited, and only deleted from the expenses list. They should be editable + deletable on the settlement page, where they belong. Prereq for BUG-1's 'route to settlement to edit' flow.
chores.plan: New updatePartnerDebt action (mirror addPartnerDebt + IDOR-safe updateForUser; preserve paidBy:'gf'/cardId:null/isShared:false/yourPercentage:1/actualExpenditure=amount/no subcategory). PartnerDebtForm gains an edit mode (optional debt prop, prefill via getExpenseForEdit, 'Save changes'). New client SettlementJournalInteractive adds edit + delete (reuse deleteExpense) dialogs on partner_debt rows; router.refresh after. Out of scope: transfers/funded-card/your_expense rows, card-payment editing, all expenses-screen changes (that's BUG-1).

chores.title: Decouple partner money from cards — drop the funded-card-payment flag
chores.description: The 'card payment funded by Brenda' flag encodes two real events as one and spawned the edge cases you hit (money-in vs money-in-then-card vs kept-as-cash). Per the model, money from Brenda is just money in; a card payment is just a card payment; they're never coupled.
chores.plan: Foundation (depends on CHORE-4). (1) Remove Movement.fundedByPartner + the 'funded_card_payment' settlement journal kind. (2) Money-from-Brenda = a plain gf_received transfer-in; a card payment = a plain card_payment — two independent events. (3) settlement.service: moneyPartnerPaidYou = Σ gf_received only (drop the fundedByPartner term from the sum). (4) Simplify the settlement help / info-icon copy now that the edge cases are gone. (5) Prod data migration: existing fundedByPartner card payments → split into a gf_received (money in) + a plain card_payment (reviewed SQL on real data).

chores.title: "I owe {partner}" becomes settlement-only — not an expense (reverses ADR-0018)
chores.description: Today 'I owe Brenda' is stored as Expense{paidBy:'gf'}, so it pollutes the expenses list, categories, budget, and spend-by-card (that's BUG-1's phantom Cash). Per the agreed cash-basis model, a thing she fronted is HER spending and only a debt to you — it must live ONLY in settlement, never in your expenses/consumption. Foundation of the money-model rework.
chores.plan: Reverses ADR-0018 — write the superseding ADR + short spec first (docs/decisions is protected: get sign-off). Then: (1) store the debt as a Movement (new type 'gf_fronted' — she fronted, you owe; amount/date/note, no card) instead of an Expense; addPartnerDebt/updatePartnerDebt write the Movement. (2) settlement.service: yourDebtToPartner = Σ gf_fronted movements (was Σ actualExpenditure of paidBy:'gf'); the journal 'partner_debt' kind reads from movements; re-point CHORE-1's edit/delete onto the movement. (3) Drop paidBy:'gf' from every read — expenses list, dashboard categories/radar/buckets, getCardSpends (phantom gone for free); deprecate Expense.paidBy (all 'you'). (4) Prod data migration: convert existing paidBy:'gf' expenses → gf_fronted movements (real data — Neon MCP survey + reviewed SQL, careful). Retires BUG-1 and the whole 'debt shows as an expense' confusion.

chores.title: Edit + delete money movements (card payments + transfers), with optional note
chores.description: Card payments and settlement transfers are delete-only (delete + re-add), and transfers can't be edited at all — surfaced when a $350 payment was logged as $320 with no way to fix it. Make both editable + deletable, mirroring CHORE-1's settlement-debt edit, and give transfers an optional note. Least urgent of the rework.
chores.plan: New updateMovement action (IDOR-safe, scoped by userId). Edit mode on the movement forms (card-payment form + transfer form); optional free-text note on transfers ('what it was toward' — a label, never a budget category). Reuse CHORE-1's dialog pattern in the settlement journal + the expenses feed. Depends on CHORE-3 (final transfer shape after the funded-card flag is gone).
